high.mackenzie.snowflake

Class AbstractTreeNode

  • java.lang.Object
    • high.mackenzie.snowflake.AbstractTreeNode
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ITreeNode, java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode>
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    TreeNode

    public abstract class AbstractTreeNode
extends java.lang.Object
implements ITreeNode
    An instance of this class is a node in a syntax-tree.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      AbstractTreeNode() 

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      ITreeNode childAt(int index)
      This method returns the child-node of this node at a given index.
      int childCount()
      This method returns the number of children of this node.
      abstract ITreeNode[] children()
      This method returns an array that contains the children of this node.
      static ITreeNode find(java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterable, java.lang.String name)
      This method searches an iterable for a particular tree-node.
      static java.util.List<ITreeNode> findAll(java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterable, java.lang.String name)
      This method searches an iterable for a particular group of tree-nodes.
      abstract char[] input()
      This method returns the char[] that is the parser's input.
      java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableBFS()
      This method returns a breadth-first-search iterable over the tree rooted at this node.
      java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableDFS()
      This method returns a depth-first-search iterable over the tree rooted at this node.
      java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableLeavesFirst()
      This method returns a leaves-first iterable over the tree rooted at this node.
      java.util.Iterator<ITreeNode> iterator()
      This method returns a depth-first-search iterator over the tree rooted at this node.
      abstract int length()
      This method returns the length of the region of the input that this node covers.
      abstract java.lang.String rule()
      This method returns the name of the rule that created this node.
      abstract int start()
      This method returns the start index of the region of the input that this node covers.
      java.lang.String text()
      This method returns the text from the matched region of input that this node covers.
      java.lang.String toString()

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractTreeNode

        public AbstractTreeNode()

    • Method Detail

      • children

        public abstract ITreeNode[] children()
        This method returns an array that contains the children of this node.

        Warning: Do NOT modify the returned array!

        Specified by:
        children in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the children of this node, or an empty array, if this node has no children.

      • rule

        public abstract java.lang.String rule()
        This method returns the name of the rule that created this node.
        Specified by:
        rule in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the aforedescribed name.

      • start

        public abstract int start()
        This method returns the start index of the region of the input that this node covers.
        Specified by:
        start in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the index of the first character in the matched region of the parser's input.

      • length

        public abstract int length()
        This method returns the length of the region of the input that this node covers.
        Specified by:
        length in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the length of the matched region of the parser's input.

      • input

        public abstract char[] input()
        This method returns the char[] that is the parser's input.

        Warning: Do NOT modify the returned array.

        Specified by:
        input in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the char[] that backs this object.

      • text

        public java.lang.String text()
        This method returns the text from the matched region of input that this node covers.
        Specified by:
        text in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the textual form of the region of input that is covered by this node.

      • childAt

        public ITreeNode childAt(int index)
        This method returns the child-node of this node at a given index.

        Zero is the index of the left-most child of this node, if this node has children.

        Specified by:
        childAt in interface ITreeNode
        Parameters:
        index - is the index of the child-node to return.
        Returns:
        the child-node at index in the array of children.

      • childCount

        public int childCount()
        This method returns the number of children of this node.
        Specified by:
        childCount in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the number of children that this node has, or zero, if this node is a leaf node.

      • iterableDFS

        public java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableDFS()
        This method returns a depth-first-search iterable over the tree rooted at this node.
        Specified by:
        iterableDFS in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the aforedescribed iterator.

      • iterableBFS

        public java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableBFS()
        This method returns a breadth-first-search iterable over the tree rooted at this node.
        Specified by:
        iterableBFS in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the aforedescribed iterator.

      • iterableLeavesFirst

        public java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterableLeavesFirst()
        This method returns a leaves-first iterable over the tree rooted at this node.

        In other words, this iterator visits all-leafs nodes first, from left to right.

        Specified by:
        iterableLeavesFirst in interface ITreeNode
        Returns:
        the aforedescribed iterator.

      • iterator

        public java.util.Iterator<ITreeNode> iterator()
        This method returns a depth-first-search iterator over the tree rooted at this node.
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface ITreeNode
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode>
        Returns:
        iteratorDFS()

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Specified by:
        toString in interface ITreeNode
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object

      • find

        public static ITreeNode find(java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterable,
             java.lang.String name)
        This method searches an iterable for a particular tree-node.
        Parameters:
        iterable - is the iterable to search.
        name - is the name of the rule that created the sought after tree-node.
        Returns:
        the first acceptable tree-node, or null, if no such tree-node is found.

      • findAll

        public static java.util.List<ITreeNode> findAll(java.lang.Iterable<ITreeNode> iterable,
                                java.lang.String name)
        This method searches an iterable for a particular group of tree-nodes.
        Parameters:
        iterable - is the iterable to search.
        name - is the name of the rule that created the sought after tree-nodes.
        Returns:
        the found tree-nodes in the order they were found, or an empty list, if no such tree-nodes are found.
